mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.0%, it signals a labor market inflection point that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate revenue forecasts and lifts S&P 500 prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative Rank IC when either HOUST or INDPRO shows negative month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations combined with a low federal funds rate environment compress real yields and trigger equity risk premium repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS satisfy the trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing retail sales volume reflects stronger consumer spending, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS changes remains below 0.01 across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth signals capacity utilization recovery and supply chain normalization, which improves profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases where INDPRO growth exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market tightening, which reduces wage-price spiral fears and boosts equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns when payroll growth exceeds 150k but unemployment rises.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth paired with rising housing starts confirms robust aggregate demand, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% but housing starts decline.
